statuses.text:
$周生生(00116)$ 

作为一个特别喜欢研究历史的人，这两日读到一篇很不错的文章，分析周生生。写于2009年2月。在那个兵荒马乱的时节，能够非常冷静审慎地看待一些投资标的，本身就是很不简单的。

网页链接

针对作者的文章作几点反思。

作者在2008年10月14日写了第一篇，叫“周生生，好得不可思议“。当时周生生最低价为3.4HKD。作者的第二篇文章写于2009年2月，当时周生生的最低价为4.23HKD。同期，标普跌到了金融危机的最低点（严格说是3月跌到的），但注意，恒生指数的最低点10676.3是2008年十月份到的。作者从08年的10月到09年的2月，显然有一个较大的态度转变。这个转变主要来自于一个原因--金价。

作者认为，金价如果跌到500USD，周生生的正常化PE应该是10倍；如果金价停留在900USD，周生生正常化PE是4倍，那么是低估的。如果金价到了1200USD，周生生的股东会发财。

我不知道作者后来是什么时候卖的，但根据他会更新标的的习惯来看，他可能09年后期就抛售了这只标的。

2011年8月，周生生的股价，飙升到了32HKD，是作者第一次写文章时的10倍。用林奇的话说，这是一只Ten-bagger。这是因为金价没有下跌，而是涨到了1800美元/oz的高位。

作者可能对贵金属还是外行了一些，因为他在所有的分析中，只字未提一个关键性变量--美联储的利率。事实上，尽管当时很多投资者认为利率会正常化，但短期利率压抑配合QE，应该是黄金价格上涨的明显前兆。当然，我是站在后视镜里看问题，而作者是雾里看花，到底有诸多不确定性。但另一个关键性因素是，即使金价下跌，需求可能增加，毛利依然是金价的30%（加工费），而且总体来说，当时中国的珠宝渗透率不到日美的40%，所以尽管短期来看会有一定周期性，但从长远看周生生，$周大福(01929)$ ，六福集团，和$老凤祥(SH600612)$ 都会把饼做大的。

当时周生生的市净率是0.50，市盈率是4倍，股息率是10%。这是一连串令人咋舌的数字。如果说市场有所顾忌，唯一的可能就是市场认为这场衰退可能变成连绵不绝的萧条--毕竟，身陷其中，无人能够知晓。当从量化角度来说，按照市价清算掉周生生，价格也不止7HKD。这就是典型的“别人恐慌我贪婪”了。

当然，没有人能够逃顶或抄底。如果作者按照09年“较高”的价格4.23HKD买入，如今的周生生价格是13.34HKD，加上这些年的分红一共是5.28HKD，共计18.62HKD，年化收益15.97%，也不赖。但作者在分析中又犯了一个错误--他误判了珠宝行业直营和代理/经销两种模式的增长速度。作者认为老凤祥和老庙（豫园股份）无法与周生生竞争，而且当时周生生在大陆只有108家店，天花板很高，增长空间很大，叠加品牌新颖，渠道控制能力强，港澳通带来的大量买家，所以赛道要比经销更好。作者某些地方说的是对的，比如周生生在一线城市有优势，但二三线城市恰好是后来十年的发展重点，而通过代理和经销来布局的老凤祥就是依靠这些较早的布点来取得先行者优势，做大自己的势力范围的。周生生的发展，其实有过于保守而错失商机之嫌（从资产负债的角度来讲也是如此）。不过需要再次重申的是，回头看永远是容易的，我更佩服那些敢于说出自己的预判的投资人。

这位作者当年是38岁，如今应该已经快48岁了。他的很多文章和文字对我都很有启发，希望他和他的妻子，已经都实现了财务自由吧！

statuses.title: 页岩油领域的巨人们
statuses.description:
在每个时代，都有一群风头正劲的人，引领彼时之潮流，乘风破浪一往无前。

statuses.text:
$AT&T(T)$ 不妨复盘思考一下AT&T的并购。如果我们回溯AT&T当时收购DirecTV（490亿，30%现金，70%股权，7.7x LTM EV/EBITDA）的时候的两个原因，一个是自身付费电视业务薄弱，U-verse的用户只有500万人，而收购DirecTV则可以将付费客户数量增加到2600万人，变成全美第三大。规模能够带来更强的议价能力，获取更廉价的内容。其次当时AT&T的高管看到的是越来越多手机用户喜欢用手机浏览内容，因此认为可以通过某种捆绑的形式去销售内容，合并无线手机端内容以及付费电视内容。后来这比并购市场基本已经判了死刑，Verizon没有大举举债并购，市值已经超越了AT&T，而AT&T因为DirecTV和时代华纳(Time Warner)的收购（后者收购为850亿, LTM EV/Revenue=3.3, LTM EV/EBITDA=11.5），负上了1700亿的债务，而Verizon的负债只有AT&T的60%。为什么失败呢？针对上述两个主要的并购逻辑，第一，AT&T小看了剪线浪潮（Cord Cutting），而DirecTV和Dish一样，都是卫星电视，不像康卡斯特(Comcast)和查特通信(Charter Communications)有宽带的保护，所以剪线造成的营业额损失远远超过自己流媒体实现的用户增加（事实上流媒体刚开始肯定不赚钱，必须要有足够的订阅用户才能具有规模经济效应，而现在DirecTV Now的用户不过200万，Netflix超过1.5亿，Hulu达到2300万，可能比惨的话，只有Dish的Sling用户数更少一些（今年增加数量才44万），这也体现到了$视频网络(DISH)$ 悲催的股价上。AT&T DirecTV的内容订阅费用是最高的，120美元/月。去年一年损失140万客户，相当于接近20亿美金的营业额。AT&T买DirecTV也是希望获得成本协同后增加自由现金流，但正如他的管理层坦诚，他们14年5月宣布这个战略的时候，尽管已经知道剪线的问题，但严重低估了其严重性。第二，AT&T高管当时设想的那种捆绑内容销售完全没有合理性，因为住户不搬家，不太会考虑换有线电视供应商，和手机选择无线电承载商的习惯完全不同，所以这两者的协同销售也没有实现。AT&T又花大价钱买了时代华纳，时代华纳的皇冠宝石是HBO，HBO曾经是可能与奈非($奈飞(NFLX)$ 竞争的，但奈非和亚马逊可以不追求稳定现金流，时代华纳不可以，AT&T更不可以。奈非为了内容而付出的资本开支可以上百亿，而且还会因为自我加强反馈系统不断进一步增加，而如今的HBO内容方面资本开支不过20-30亿，AT&T还需要承担高昂的分红(6.7%的股息，去年一年127亿美金）以及高额债务造成的利息支出（去年一年77亿美金），而AT&T一年的自由现金流不过200亿美金，扣除分红后，如果不还债，能够内生增长的也就是60几亿美金，其拥有的地面网络为了适应5G的进一步发展还需要许多资本开支，能用在HBO和DirecTV Now上的更是寥寥无几。毕竟AT&T和NFLX的持股股东群体是截然不同的。尽管时代华纳的收购在一定程度上有内容的保护（Warner Bro，CNN，etc），但TWX当时的收购价格，可能和DirecTV一样，都被高估了，而且会随着剧烈变化的投资环境进一步丧失竞争能力。

不妨延伸思考：康卡斯特(Comcast)以比迪斯尼(Disney)收购福克斯(Fox)天空网络(Sky)股权更高的价格进入英国市场，而天空网络恰是卫星电视，康卡斯特出价又是如此高昂，是否有点deja vu的感觉呢？而当年因为AT&T收购DirecTV而变成了孤家寡人的Dish，在Sling TV萎靡不振，自己卫星电视业务又急剧萎缩的当下，估值仍然有10.8x LTM EV/EBITDA（150亿长短债，138亿市值，27亿EBITDA），出路又在哪里呢？

statuses.description:

第二天，用自己的很小一部分资金买入了这些标...

statuses.text: $纽威品牌(NWL)$ 低估得夸张了，从20美元/股一路买到17美元/股，屯仓完毕，稍微写了点notes，等有空再写点中文版的吧... Newell restructured its business into Learning & Development, which includes baby & writing, famous brands include Baby Jogger and Graco. This section has about 3.3 billion dollars of revenue and an operating margin of 16.4%; Home & Outdoor Living, including connected home & security such as Onelink, home fragrance such as Yankee Candle, and Outdoor & Recreation such as Coleman. This section has revenue of 3.1 billion dollars and an operating margin of 8.8%; Food & Appliances which includes appliances & cookware, food such as Oster. This section has revnue of 2.9 billion dollars, and an operating margin of 10.7%. Other renowned brands include Ball -- wide mouth quart sharing jars, Sharpie Marker, Elmer's Ooey Gluey Slime Games for student scientists, etc. They are also innovating on a wide range of products. These products, unlike Kraft Foods or Coca Cola, do not suffer any health-related headwind. They are also less likely to be impacted by e-commerce. Many brands are more than a hundred years old, and I think in another hundred years they will still be relevant, such as Rubbermaid, Yankee Candle, and people are still going to go outdoor with Coleman products. The company is depressed for four reasons. Firstly, Toys 'R U's liquidation resulted in cheap selling of their products through TRU's channels, depressing margin. This is one-time, and I believe the company will quickly shift its product to online sources; Secondly, writing channel inventory -- the writing channel are reducing their inventory, naturally sales from Newell is constrained. These two channels turned out to be most relevant for NWL because of this section's highest operating margin, but I believe both are one-time headwind. The third one is the de-leveraging process. They sold Waddington for 2.3 billion at a 12x EBITDA which is not bad. This is originally a deleveraging story -- they bought a variety of companies such as Jarden and wasn't able to digest, also they have a shit suit of debt, operating profit suffered and now they wanted to divest and pay down their debt. Their normalized ROE is around 15% and I think this is sustainable given their products are all dominant brands in their respective niche markets -- coupling economy of scale, brand-awareness induced customer captivity through habit and search costs as major barriers of entry. They have pretty good moat. Their debt on average costs 4-5%. As they delever, equity holder will benefit. They reduced net debt by end of Q2 by 2.4 billion, gross debt 900 million. Now they have about 9.8 billion gross debt. Assuming 12% operating margin, they have more than 2x coverage ratio, so financial problem caused by debt is lessened (on a net debt level coverage ratio is perhaps closer to 3x). So deleverage is going well. By end of 2019 they will divest a total of 10 billion. Their normalized EPS low-end is $2.45, operating cash is 0.9-1.2 billion. operating cash yield is around 9.2% to 12.2%, and current price implied a PE of 8.16x even taking the lower end of the guidance. This is cheap for a sustainable, wide moat company. It should be able to grow its core at 5% (faster than global growth of 3-4%), and now has an earnings yield of 4.6%, and when it expands in multiple, I think it deserves a 15 times multiple given the strong brand once the deleverage is completed. Assuming, conservatively, it takes 5-10 years to realize that, then the stock will have a CAGR of 6.2%-12.9% simply due to multiple expansion. This implies a 15.8% - 22.5% CAGR for the next five years. Plus, the company is buying back its own shares, and that should imply higher upside for the equity price. One last problem is tariff, it will impact close to 3 billion dollar of sales (for a total of close to 10 billion dollar of revenue). It will have a negative impact of as much as 100 million, given 488 million outstanding shares, it will imply 20.5 cents of cost eating into earnings. Remember we used conservative earnings estimate of $2.45. Let's just assume it goes to $2.2/share, this corresponds to 9.1x PE. We recalculate the annualized return to be 15.7%-21.7% of CAGR for the stock price, not considering buy back. Under such a conservative scenario, price should be trading around $39.7~ $50/share in the October of 2023. With Carl Icahn and Starboard in the game, value should be unleashed, and investor has great guardians that closely watch for any corporate misbehavior. I personally like the niche dominance, brand awareness, economy of scale, customer captivity among these brands, certainly in growth opportunities on an international scale, and last but not least, great staying power due to these competitive advantages.

statuses.text: 汇总一下一些宏观看法。三位哈佛大学经济系的教授（其中一位曾是我老师）以及一位普林斯顿大学的经济系教授在今年6月NBER发了一篇文章，叫做the Macroeconomics of Border Taxes，喜欢宏观经济体系建模的朋友可以关注一下。他们用动态基本均衡推出的结论总结起来是：企业税务改革是宏观非中性的，永久性降低企业税20%将导致等值的美元货币升值，进出口将大幅下降，总体对产出影响较小。关税的增加对美元升值影响较小（注意，他们没有提到人民币贬值压力会有多大），进出口微微减小，但长期来看对产出的负面影响显著。贸易战确实会在短期，尤其是政府赤字的前提下增加美国政府收入，但基于美国的净外资处境，从长期来看，政府收入将受损，且此收入将流向世界其他部分。JP Morgan的Jamie Dimon认为，减税有利于产出增加（这个观点与几位教授不完全一致），但关税一定会导致长期增长受损（这点与几位教授一致）。几位教授基于严密数理经济模型得出的结论与周小川上周在意大利的判断基本一致，周小川还认为美国就算加5000亿美金的关税，影响也很小，这部分货物将被中国非常高效地运往世界其他地区。这一点查理芒格先生也提到了，美国没有意识到贸易不是双向，而是多边的，孤立中国，只是将中国的贸易引导到别的国家去罢了，从系统的角度看，这叫做homeostasis。明星基金经理David Tepper在四天前CNBC的访谈中提到贸易战短期会对美国经济造成影响，但中国剽窃美国科技的行为必须被制止。他认为美国如果施加关税，央妈若不干预，在静态分析下人民币会贬值到7点几，甚至8，他说这是简单的数学计算（尽管我其实并不清楚他脑子里计算出这个结论的是什么模型，如果有了解的，请指教）。我在这里拷贝黏贴一下几位教授的文献的摘要：We analyze the dynamic macroeconomic effects of border adjustment taxes, both when they are a feature of corporate tax reform (C-BAT) and for the case of value added taxes (VAT). Our analysis arrives at the following main conclusions. First, C-BAT is unlikely to be neutral at the macroeconomic level, as the conditions required for neutrality are unrealistic. The basis for neutrality of VAT is even weaker. Second, in response to the introduction of an unanticipated permanent C-BAT of 20% in the U.S. the dollar appreciates strongly, by almost the size of the tax adjustment, U.S. exports and imports decline significantly, while the overall effect on output is small. Third, an equivalent change in VAT by contrast generates only a weak appreciation of the dollar, a small decline in imports and exports, but has a large negative effect on output. Lastly, border taxes increase government revenues in periods of trade deficit, however, given the net foreign asset position of the U.S., they result in a long-run loss of government revenues and an immediate net transfer to the rest of the world.

statuses.text:
布鲁斯法拉特，亦可赛艇！中国，一个遍地黄金的地方！~

In population and GDP, “China is going to be a major driver of global growth,” Flatt says. “By and large, China’s economic transition has been done in a methodical way such that it’s a place where we feel comfortable investing.”

About 1% of Brookfield’s assets -- a few billion dollars – are currently invested in China. That’s now going to change. In Flatt’s own words, “The bottom line is I have to believe 25 years from now a third of our business will be in China.” With China now at a stage of development where its acceptance of foreign capital is on the rise, the firm is ready to act. Says Flatt, “We’ve spent nearly 15 years building out our business in the Asia/Pacific region. We're now at a point where we can do things in China."

“In the early days, China was a hyper-growth market and that's something that we're not generally comfortable with,” says Flatt. “Today, China is forming into an economy that's more like the developed economies. The investment opportunities become more value and operationally based," he adds. As a tested manager of everything from New York's World Financial Center to London's Canary Wharf, Flatt says, "Our operating skills can be highly beneficial to businesses in the country."

“Thirty-five years from now half of global GDP will be in Asia and half will be in the Western economy," says Flatt. “Our belief is China will be a great place to invest."

statuses.text:
Words from Bruce Greenwald's "Final Lecture"

1. One of the most important things to teach people is to learn what your own mistakes are. You have to understand that you’re going to make them again and again. You have to learn about who you are. That’s hard for kids who come in thinking they’re sophisticated investors by the time they’re 26 years old. It’s a mixed benefit that they’re more sophisticated.

2. The hardest challenges are not industry challenges, because where industries are a challenge, it’s almost always where somebody like Amazon has come in and grabbed share.

Well, if somebody can come in and grab share, it’s not a stable market yet. In disruptive industries, before the disruption settles down, there are not going to be barriers to entry because the customers are not going to be captive since the product is changing so much. And the market is going to be changing so much that it’s very hard to secure scale.

3. So, I think the hardest thing for people to get their heads around is management and what they can do to you.

Especially if they don’t understand where their competitive advantage in business is. When that plays out, a guy who used to be a good manager, like Michael Dell, is not a good manager while the industry changes.

It’s the interaction of the management with changing circumstances that’s hard to chart.

4. There would be hiccups along the way, especially when a company goes through a manufacturing transition. But from our perspective, we had a cheap, cyclically depressed company with secular tailwinds and a management team focused on the things they could control, such as improving their competitive advantage in manufacturing and making money in all parts of the cycle.

It took them six years to fully implement the changes. The cycle kicked in about two years later, and Deere was making so much equipment that it took longer than expected to slow production to retool their factories.

statuses.text: $Charter电信(CHTR)$ $Charter电信(CHTR)$ 太好了，全美最好Cable Operator Tom Rutledge的公司，Integration On Track，Internet Subscriber增长33w，所有financial metrics全部in line，EBITDA增长6.5%，Revenue增长4.9%，除了Voice之外所有其他sector全部增长，几乎毫无差错地执行着Bright House和Time Warner Cable的整合工作，去年8月孙正义还想用Sprint的股票和现金作价540美元/股收购全公司，被董事席否决 -- 就是这样一家企业，今天大甩卖了。八年十倍，股价高高在上几乎从不回头，从一家破产重组后的垃圾企业被Tom一手转变成全美第二大的有线电视运营商。感谢损失的那12w video subscriber以及短暂的资本开支增加导致的单季负自由现金流创造了这次自从上市以来最大的sale-off。公司该季度回购6亿美金，债务比EBITDA进一步下降，渗透率进一步增加。还是那句话--高位单数的EBITDA增长加上适当的杠杆加上NOL加上股票回购，以任何EBITDA基数为基点，应用一个以3-5年为前瞻单位的合适乘数，价值都会有巨大的增加。买入
